name: Akka Mahadevi
summary: Akka Mahadevi was an early poet of Kannada literature and a prominent member of the Lingayatism denomination of Hinduism founded in the 12th century. Her 430 vachanas, and the two short writings called Mantrogopya and the Yogangatrividh are considered her known contributions to Kannada literature. The term Akka was an honorific given to her by saints such as Basavanna, Siddharama, and Allamaprabhu as an indication of her high place in the spiritual discussions held at the 'Anubhava Mantapa'. She is regarded to be a major female figure in Kannada literature and in the history of Karnataka. She considered the god Shiva as her husband.

name: B.K. Sumitra
summary: Bilalukoppa Krishnayya Sumitra, popularly known as B. K. Sumitra, is an Indian singer known for her work in Kannada films. She is also popular for her numerous devotional and folk songs. She has contributed significantly to the 'Sugama Sangeetha' (light music) genre in Karnataka.

name: Basavanna
summary: Basava (1131–1196), also called Basavēśvara and Basavaṇṇā, was an Indian philosopher, poet, Lingayat social reformer in the Shiva-focused bhakti movement, and a Hindu Shaivite social reformer during the reign of the Kalyani Chalukya and the Kalachuri Dynasty. Basava was active during the rule of both dynasties, but his influence peaked during the reign of King Bijjala II in Karnataka, India. He is known for his Vachanas, which spread social awareness and equality through simple poetry.

name: Channa Basavanna
summary: Channabasavanna also known as 'Guru Channabasaveshwara' was Basava's nephew and one of the foremost Sharanas of the 12th century. He, along with Basava, Allama Prabhu and Akka Mahadevi, played a pivotal role in the propagation of the Lingayat faith. He was the youngest among the sharana leaders and grew up in the household of Basavanna. He also wrote the Karana Hasuge which is one of the most sacred texts of the Lingayats, among many vachanas. He propounded the 'shatasthala' philosophy associated with the six holy places of Veerashaiva Lingayat creed.

name: Chi. Udayashankar
summary: Chitnahalli Udayashankar was an Indian lyricist and dialogue writer in the Kannada film industry for over three decades, who penned more than 3000 songs for films and devotional songs. His dialogues, screenplay and lyrics were instrumental in the success of many movies of Rajkumar. Udayashankar received four Karnataka State Film Award for Best Dialogue and two Karnataka State Film Award for Best Screenplay. He is often called 'Sahitya Rathna' for his immense contribution to Kannada cinema lyrics.

name: D.R. Bendre
summary: Dattātreya Rāmachandra Bēndre, popularly known as Da Rā Bēndre, is generally considered the greatest Kannada lyric poet of the 20th century and one of the greatest poets in the history of Kannada literature. A pioneering poet of Kannada's Navōdaya movement and a leading figure in the linguistic renaissance of Kannada. Bendre was awarded the Jnanpith Award, the highest literary honor in India, for his collection 'Naaku Tanti'. He was hailed as a 'Varakavi' (gifted poet) for his ability to weave magic with simple Kannada words.

name: D.V. Gundappa
summary: Devanahalli Venkataramanaiah Gundappa, popularly known as DVG, was an Indian writer, poet and philosopher in Kannada-language. He is one of the stalwarts of modern Kannada literature. His most notable work is the 'Mankuthimmana Kagga', a collection of 945 four-line poems that are considered the 'Bhagavad Gita' of modern Kannada life. DVG was a scholar who blended ancient wisdom with modern critical thinking.

name: Folk
summary: Kannada Folk literature (Janapada) is a rich tradition of oral literature passed down through generations. It includes folk songs (Tripadi), ballads, and tales that reflect the cultural heritage, struggles, and joys of the rural people of Karnataka. These songs are often associated with farming, festivals, and life-cycle events, preserving the raw essence of Kannada culture without formal authorship.

name: G.S. Shivarudrappa
summary: Guggari Shanthaveerappa Shivarudrappa, or colloquially GSS, was an Indian Kannada poet, writer, and researcher who was awarded the title of Rashtrakavi by the Government of Karnataka in 2006. He was a prominent figure in the 'Samanvaya' (Synthesized) movement in Kannada literature. His works like 'Saakshi' and 'Kaavyartha Chintana' are milestones in Kannada poetry and criticism.

name: Gopalakrishna Adiga
summary: Mogeri Gopalakrishna Adiga (1918–1992) was a modern Kannada poet. He is known as the pioneer of the 'Navya' (Modernist) style of poetry. His work 'Bhoomi Geetha' is considered a landmark in modern Kannada literature. Adiga brought a new intellectual depth and complex imagery to Kannada poetry, reflecting the post-independence disillusionment and search for identity.

name: Jayant Kaikini
summary: Jayant Kaikini is a contemporary poet, short story writer, playwright, and columnist in Kannada, as well as a popular lyricist in Kannada cinema. He has revolutionized Kannada film music with his poetic and soulful lyrics. He has won several Karnataka Sahitya Academy awards and is known for his ability to find beauty in the mundane details of urban life.

name: K.S. Narasimhaswamy
summary: Kikkeri Subbarao Narasimhaswamy was a celebrated Indian poet who wrote in the Kannada language. His most popular collection of poems, 'Mysooru Mallige', is a household name in Karnataka, depicting the romance and simple joys of married life. He is often called 'Mallige Kavi' for the sweetness and fragrance of his romantic poetry.

name: K.S. Nissar Ahmed
summary: Kokkare Hosahalli Shekh Haider Nissar Ahmed was a legendary Indian poet and writer in the Kannada language. He became a household name for his poem 'Nityotsava', a vibrant celebration of Karnataka's landscape. He was awarded the Padma Shri and the Pampa Award. His poetry often reflected a deep love for the Kannada land and its people, bridging cultural divides.

name: Kanakadasa
summary: Kanaka Dasa (1509–1606) was a renowned Haridasa saint, philosopher, and composer from Karnataka. He belonged to the Dasa Sahitya movement and was a disciple of Vyasatirtha. His keertanas often addressed social issues like caste discrimination and preached the path of devotion (Bhakti). His work 'Nala Charitre' and 'Mohana Tarangini' are highly regarded in Kannada literature.

name: Kiran Kaverappa
summary: Kiran Kaverappa is a contemporary Kannada lyricist known for his work in Kannada cinema. He has written lyrics for several popular songs in films like 'Bell Bottom', 'Dia', and 'Kirik Party'. His lyrics often blend traditional Kannada metaphors with modern sensibilities, making him a favorite among the younger generation of listeners.

name: Kuvempu
summary: Kuppalli Venkatappa Puttappa, popularly known as Kuvempu, was an Indian poet, playwright, novelist, and critic. He is widely regarded as the greatest Kannada poet of the 20th century. He was the first Kannada writer to receive the Jnanpith Award for his epic 'Sri Ramayana Darshanam'. He also wrote the state anthem of Karnataka, 'Jaya Bharata Jananiya Tanujate'.

name: N.S. Lakshminarayana Bhatta
summary: Nilathahalli Venkatasubbaiah Lakshminarayana Bhatta (1936 – 2021) was an Indian Kannada poet, writer and academic. He was a popular poet of the 'Navya' (Modernist) movement. He is well-known for his translation of William Shakespeare's sonnets and the works of T. S. Eliot. His poetry often explored the nuances of human emotions and urban life with a unique musicality.

name: Purandara Dasa
summary: Purandara Dasa was a legendary composer, singer and a Haridasa philosopher. He is referred to as the 'Pitamaha' (grandfather) of Carnatic music due to his fundamental contributions to the system of Carnatic music. He was a wealthy merchant who gave up his worldly possessions to become a wandering devotee of Lord Vitthala. His thousands of keertanas are filled with spiritual wisdom and social commentary.

name: Sathyananda Pathrota
summary: Dr. Sathyananda Pathrota is a renowned Kannada poet, playwright, and activist, popularly known as the 'Jaji Mallige Kavi'. He is celebrated for his powerful expression of the struggles and aspirations of the oppressed communities. His poem 'Badavanadare Enu Priye' became a massive hit across Karnataka. He was awarded the Karnataka Rajyotsava Award for his contributions to literature.

name: Shishunala Sharif
summary: Santha Shishunala Sharif (1819–1889) was a celebrated Indian saint, social reformer, philosopher, and poet. He is often called the 'Kabir of Karnataka'. His compositions (Tatvapada) are known for their profound philosophical meaning expressed through simple folk metaphors. He was a disciple of Guru Govinda Bhatta, and their relationship is a legendary example of communal harmony.

name: V. Anand
summary: V. Anand is a respected Kannada lyricist who has contributed beautiful verses to the world of Sugama Sangeetha (light music). He is best known for the evergreen song 'Yaava Mohana Murali Kareyitu', which was immortalized by the voice of P. Kalinga Rao. His poetry is characterized by a deep sense of nostalgia and philosophical longing.
